excerpt: NSA collection summary and reviewed pages indicate a 4,000-6,000 Dacca total from military action by 31 March.
transform_note: Used as the citywide direct-death envelope from which status-specific estimates are derived.
confidence_note: Strongest single near-contemporaneous Dacca total found, but still an early estimate.

excerpt: Rogers relayed the Dacca consul general's estimate of 4,000-6,000 killed in the Dacca area.
transform_note: Used as corroboration of the NSA/consular citywide total.
confidence_note: Corroborating aggregate source; still all-status and early.

excerpt: The cable gives a conservative 500 and an upper range of 1,000 for students killed at the university.
transform_note: Placed in unknown status rather than civilian because other sources report some armed resistance or weapons at the halls.
confidence_note: Range is direct, but status is contested and probably heterogeneous.

excerpt: The cable reports 600-800 East Pakistani police killed during the hard fighting on the night of 25 March.
transform_note: Mapped to military/combatant per project operational definition for police used as combat force.
confidence_note: Strong direct subclaim, but still an early source.

excerpt: A cable citing an EPR survivor reported 700 killed out of about 1,000 present.
transform_note: Used cautiously because this is a survivor-based sub-report rather than a systematic roll.
confidence_note: Valuable but weaker than the police range because it depends on one reported survivor account.

excerpt: Bose reproduces both radio-traffic claims of incoming fire and a Simon Dring passage saying students had only a few rifles and apparently did not use them.
transform_note: Used only to justify a conservative unknown-status bucket for some university victims; not used as a casualty count.
confidence_note: Interpretively contested source, used narrowly and with caution.

excerpt: Salik frames the episode as a city-and-suburbs operation, supporting a city-level denominator footprint.
confidence_note: Useful theater statement from a participant narrative.

excerpt: Farman's Dacca task list names four West Pakistani infantry battalions, plus artillery, light anti-aircraft, and a commando company.
transform_note: Used to bound the West Pakistani assault grouping for the military denominator.
confidence_note: Strong for unit identity, weaker for exact strength.

excerpt: Farman's tasking appendix gives 2,500 for EPR HQ in Dacca.
transform_note: Used as one side of the source-disagreement band for Bengali organized-armed presence.
confidence_note: Direct but conflicts with Salik's larger figure.

excerpt: Farman's appendix gives 2,000 reserve police at Rajar Bagh.
transform_note: Used as one side of the source-disagreement band for Bengali organized-armed presence.
confidence_note: Direct but conflicts with Salik's smaller figure.

excerpt: Salik gives a much larger EPR figure than Farman for the Dacca target.
transform_note: Used to widen denominator bounds rather than averaged mechanically with Farman.
confidence_note: Important disagreement signal rather than a figure to accept uncritically.

excerpt: Salik gives 1,000 policemen at Rajarbagh, versus Farman's 2,000 reserve police.
transform_note: Used to widen denominator bounds rather than averaged mechanically with Farman.
confidence_note: Important disagreement signal rather than a figure to accept uncritically.

excerpt: Sukhwant Singh notes that at least some West Pakistani battalions in the east were thin, at about 400 strength.
transform_note: Used only as a low-end strength anchor for West Pakistani battalions in the denominator scenario.

excerpt: Supports the proposition that Dacca had recent reinforcements but some were still understrength.
transform_note: Supports denominator scenario construction; not itself the full Dhaka force count.

excerpt: The planning article reports Dhaka city population at 556,712 in 1961.
transform_note: Lower time anchor for 1971 civilian population backcast.
confidence_note: Solid census anchor but not an episode-year value.

excerpt: The article reports 1,772,438 for Dhaka in 1974 and separately notes that post-1971 growth was unusually high.
transform_note: Upper time anchor for 1971 civilian population backcast.
confidence_note: Strong anchor, but the source itself warns that 1961-1974 growth is inflated by post-liberation capital influx.

excerpt: The article explicitly warns that post-1971 capital status boosted Dhaka's growth, so 1974 should not be read straight back into March 1971.
transform_note: Used to keep the 1971 baseline below a naive linear interpolation.
confidence_note: Important qualitative constraint on the civilian denominator.

excerpt: ICJ records a rapid exodus from Dacca within days of the crackdown.
transform_note: Used to model a downward adjustment to civilian person-months over the episode.
confidence_note: Qualitative but directly relevant to civilian exposure.
